name: Speedster Hovercycle
notes: A fast all-terrain hover vehicle. Main body M.D.C.: 85, hover jets 30 each. One rider; a second passenger can ride, but not comfortably for long trips. Standard weapon options include either a laser (1D6 M.D., 1200 ft, 20 shots) or a machine-gun (1D4 M.D. per 50-round burst, 2000 ft, 600-round payload). Mini-missile launchers can be added to the sides. Cost: 98,000 credits gasoline, 110,000 electric, or 450,000 nuclear.

name: Wastelander Motorcycle
notes: A heavy-duty rough-terrain motorcycle with built-in storage. Main body M.D.C.: 60, tires 2 each. Standard weapons are not included; add 4,000 credits for a machine-gun or 11,000 credits for a laser. Cost: 18,000 credits gasoline or 21,000 electric.

name: Highway-Man Motorcycle
notes: A fast, rugged cycle common in cities and flat country. Main body M.D.C.: 75, tires 2 each. Usually sold with the same laser or machine-gun options used on the Speedster Hovercycle. Cost: 24,000 credits gasoline or 29,000 electric, plus weapon cost.

name: Big Boss ATV
notes: A popular all-terrain dune buggy. Main body M.D.C.: 100, tires 5 each. One pilot can carry three passengers comfortably. It often mounts a roof weapon with 360-degree rotation and a 90-degree arc of fire; add the weapon separately. Cost: 24,000 credits gasoline or 28,000 electric.

name: Mountaineer ATV
notes: A heavily armored all-terrain transport with a sealed pilot compartment and cargo bay. Main body M.D.C.: 210, super tires 25 each, reinforced pilot compartment 50. Carries one pilot, four passengers, and cargo. Additional armor can be added in 30 M.D.C. increments for 10,000 credits each. Cost: 76,000 credits basic gasoline, 70,000 electric, or 500,000 nuclear.

name: Wilk's Jet Pack
notes: A personal jet pack with helmet, neck support, short-range radio, and compass. One rider. No built-in weapons, but normal handguns and rifles can be used in flight. Main body M.D.C.: 30. Cost: 38,000 credits gasoline, 50,000 electric, or 350,000 nuclear.
